methods.method_name: Initial Data Preparation for State Space Analysis
methods.description: Preparation of spatiotemporal climate fields (Y) for dynamical system analysis by weighting each time series by the cosine of its latitude, centering to zero mean, and standardizing each field to unit variance. This process transforms the data into a state space vector at each time step, representing the system's state.

steps.description: For a given field Y, weight each time series by the cosine of its latitude.
steps.searchable_summary: apply latitude weighting to time series data
outputs: Latitude-weighted time series data

steps.description: Center each time series to zero mean.
steps.searchable_summary: center time series data to zero mean
outputs: Zero-mean weighted time series data

steps.description: Standardize each field to unit variance.
steps.searchable_summary: standardize data fields to unit variance
outputs: Standardized climate fields (state space vector components)

methods.method_name: Principal Component Analysis (PCA) / Empirical Orthogonal Functions (EOF)
methods.description: A linear methodology for modal decomposition of spatiotemporal datasets (X) to discover the underlying manifold by fitting hyperplanes in directions explaining most of the variance. Commonly known as Empirical Orthogonal Functions (EOF) in climate science.

steps.description: Compute the Gram matrix as G = 1/(T-1) * X * X^T from the spatiotemporal dataset X.
steps.searchable_summary: compute Gram matrix from spatiotemporal data
inputs: Spatiotemporal dataset X (T x N matrix)
outputs: Gram matrix G (T x T matrix)

steps.description: Compute the eigenvectors U and eigenvalues of the Gram matrix G.
steps.searchable_summary: compute eigenvectors and eigenvalues of a matrix
outputs: Eigenvectors U (Principal Components)
Eigenvalues

steps.description: To each eigenvector U, assign an explained variance based on the ratio of its correspondent eigenvalue and the sum of all eigenvalues.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate explained variance for each principal component
outputs: Explained variance for each Principal Component

steps.description: To identify the correspondent spatial patterns, known as EOFs, linearly regress each (standardized) PC on the dataset as 1/(T-1) * U^T * X.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate EOF spatial patterns by regressing PCs on data
outputs: Empirical Orthogonal Functions (EOFs)

methods.description: A nonlinear manifold learning algorithm used for dimensionality reduction, preserving geodesic distances between points.

steps.description: Apply Isomap for dimensionality reduction to the dataset (e.g., Surface Temperature or state space representation).
steps.searchable_summary: perform Isomap dimensionality reduction
inputs: Dataset (e.g., spatiotemporal data, state space vectors)
outputs: Low-dimensional embedding/projection of the data

steps.description: Set the neighborhood size parameter (e.g., k=50) for the Isomap projection.
steps.searchable_summary: set neighborhood size parameter for Isomap
outputs: Isomap model configured with neighborhood size

methods.method_name: Complex Network Analysis of Climate Modes
methods.description: A framework to quantify the evolution of major modes of interannual-to-decadal variability and their connectivity patterns in a system (e.g., Indo-Pacific) using a complex network representation where modes are domains and links represent connectivity.

steps.description: Identify domains corresponding to spatially contiguous sets of sea surface temperature (SST) grid cells with highly correlated temporal evolution.
steps.searchable_summary: identify spatially correlated climate domains
outputs: Defined climate domains (spatial masks)

steps.description: Given a time window, quantify the variability of each domain by computing its signal as the average monthly anomaly inside it.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate average monthly anomaly within climate domains
outputs: Time series signal for each domain within the window

steps.description: Quantify the evolution of the standard deviation of each domain signal along with their underlying correlation network in time windows of specified length and step.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate standard deviation and correlation network of domain signals over sliding windows
outputs: Evolution of standard deviation for each domain
Correlation network structure for each time window

steps.description: Given two domains, quantify connectivity by the maximum significant correlation in a specified lag range (e.g., [-12, 12] months lag range).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate maximum significant lagged correlation between domain signals
inputs: Time series signals for two domains
outputs: Maximum significant correlation value
Lag at which maximum correlation occurs

steps.description: For a given time window and for each pair of domains, consider only the link directionality appearing with the largest frequency.
steps.searchable_summary: determine dominant link directionality based on frequency over time
inputs: Lagged correlation results for domain pairs over time windows
outputs: Dominant link directionality for each domain pair in each window

steps.description: The reported frequency therefore quantifies the stability of each link.
steps.searchable_summary: quantify link stability using frequency of dominant directionality
outputs: Link stability metric

steps.description: The average lag indicates the link directionality, while the mean correlation for each pair of domains quantifies the strength of the connections.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate average lag and mean correlation for domain pairs
inputs: Lagged correlation results for domain pairs over time windows
outputs: Average lag for each domain pair
Mean correlation for each domain pair

methods.description: Qualitative comparison of Sea Surface Temperature (SST) variability between a climate model (IPSL) and reanalysis data (ERA5) by comparing the first Empirical Orthogonal Function (EOF) in the Pacific Ocean and the first two in the Indian Ocean.

steps.description: Obtain monthly SST anomalies for both model and reanalysis data.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate monthly SST anomalies
outputs: Model monthly SST anomalies
Reanalysis monthly SST anomalies

steps.description: Remove seasonality from the monthly SST anomalies.
steps.searchable_summary: remove seasonality from time series data
outputs: Seasonality-removed SST anomalies

steps.description: Remove linear trend from the seasonality-removed SST anomalies.
steps.searchable_summary: remove linear trend from time series data
outputs: Seasonality and trend-removed SST anomalies

steps.description: Compute the first Empirical Orthogonal Function (EOF) in the Pacific Ocean for both model and reanalysis data using the processed SST anomalies.
steps.searchable_summary: compute first EOF for Pacific SST anomalies
inputs: Processed SST anomalies (model and reanalysis) for Pacific region
outputs: First EOF for model Pacific SST
First EOF for reanalysis Pacific SST

steps.description: Compute the first two EOFs in the Indian Ocean for both model and reanalysis data using the processed SST anomalies.
steps.searchable_summary: compute first two EOFs for Indian Ocean SST anomalies
inputs: Processed SST anomalies (model and reanalysis) for Indian Ocean region
outputs: First two EOFs for model Indian Ocean SST
First two EOFs for reanalysis Indian Ocean SST

steps.description: Qualitatively compare the spatial patterns and explained variance of the computed EOFs between the model and reanalysis.
steps.searchable_summary: qualitatively compare model and reanalysis EOFs
outputs: Qualitative assessment of model performance in capturing observed variability modes

methods.description: Investigation of changes in the seasonality of identified climate modes (e.g., IOB, IOD, EDM) over different time periods.

steps.description: Identify or obtain time series signals/indices for relevant climate modes (e.g., IOB, IOD, EDM).
steps.searchable_summary: obtain time series indices for climate modes
inputs: Definitions of climate modes (e.g., spatial patterns, domain masks)
outputs: Time series indices for climate modes

steps.description: Analyze the seasonality of the signal/variability associated with each mode for different time periods (e.g., 100-year windows).
steps.searchable_summary: analyze seasonality of climate mode time series over time windows
inputs: Time series indices for climate modes
Time periods of interest (e.g., time windows)
outputs: Seasonality patterns for each mode in each time period

steps.description: Compare the seasonality patterns across different time periods to identify changes.
steps.searchable_summary: compare seasonality patterns of climate modes across different periods
inputs: Seasonality patterns for each mode in multiple time periods
outputs: Description or visualization of changes in seasonality over time
